name: ElmHtml.ToHtml

values.name: factsToAttributes
values.comment: Turns a fact record into a list of attributes

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Turns ElmHtml into normal Elm Html

name: ElmHtml.ToString

aliases.name: FormatOptions
aliases.comment: Formatting options to be used for converting to string

values.name: defaultFormatOptions
values.comment: default formatting options

values.name: nodeRecordToString
values.comment:
Convert a node record to a string. This basically takes the tag name, then
    pulls all the facts into tag declaration, then goes through the children and
    nests them under this one

values.name: nodeToString
values.comment: Convert a given html node to a string based on the type

values.name: nodeToStringWithOptions
values.comment: same as nodeToString, but with options

name: ElmHtml.ToElmString

aliases.name: FormatOptions
aliases.comment: Formatting options to be used for converting to string

values.name: defaultFormatOptions
values.comment: default formatting options

values.name: nodeRecordToString
values.comment:
Convert a node record to a string. This basically takes the tag name, then
pulls all the facts into tag declaration, then goes through the children and
nests them under this one

values.name: toElmString
values.comment: Convert a given html node to a string based on the type

values.name: toElmStringWithOptions
values.comment: same as toElmString, but with options

name: ElmHtml.InternalTypes

unions.name: Attribute
unions.comment:
Type for representing Elm's Attributes

  - Attribute is an HTML attribute, like `Html.Attributes.colspan`. These values
    are applied using `element.setAttribute(key, value)` during a patch.
  - NamespacedAttribute has an namespace, like `Svg.Attributes.xlinkHref`
  - Property assigns a value to a node like `Html.Attributes.class`, and can
    hold any encoded value. Unlike attributes, where `element.setAttribute()` is
    used during the patch, properties are applied directly as
    `element[key] = value`.
  - Styles hold a list of key value pairs to be applied to the node's style set
  - Event contains a decoder for a msg and the `Html.Event.Options` for the event

unions.name: ElementKind
unions.comment:
Type for representing the five kinds of elements according to HTML 5
[spec](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/syntax.html#elements-2).
Used to handle different rendering behavior depending on the type of element.

unions.name: ElmHtml
unions.comment:
Type tree for representing Elm's Html

  - TextTag is just a plain old bit of text.
  - NodeEntry is an actual HTML node, e.g a div
  - CustomNode are nodes defined to work with the renderer in some way, e.g webgl/markdown
  - MarkdownNode is just a wrapper for CustomNode designed just for markdown

aliases.name: AttributeRecord
aliases.comment: Attribute contains a string key and a string value

aliases.name: CustomNodeRecord
aliases.comment: Custom nodes contain facts (e.g attributes) and a json value for the model

aliases.name: EventHandler
EventRecord
aliases.comment: Event contains a string key, a decoder for a msg and event options
aliases.type: { key : String, decoder : Value, options : Options }

aliases.name: Facts
aliases.comment:
Facts contain various dictionaries and values for a node

  - styles are a mapping of rules
  - events may be a json object containing event handlers
  - attributes are pulled out into stringAttributes and boolAttributes - things with string values go into
    stringAttributes, things with bool values go into boolAttributes
aliases.type: { styles : Dict String String, events : Dict String (Decoder msg), attributeNamespace : Maybe Value, stringAttributes : Dict String String, boolAttributes : Dict String Bool }

aliases.name: MarkdownNodeRecord
aliases.comment: A markdown node contains facts (e.g attributes) and the model used by markdown

aliases.name: NamespacedAttributeRecord
aliases.comment: NamespacedAttribute contains a string key, string namespace and string value
aliases.type: { key : String, value : String, namespace : String }

aliases.name: NodeRecord
aliases.comment: A node contains the `tag` as a string, the children, the facts (e.g attributes) and descendantsCount
aliases.type: { tag : String, children : List (ElmHtml msg), facts : Facts msg, descendantsCount : Int }

aliases.name: PropertyRecord
aliases.comment: Property contains a string key and a value with an arbitrary type

aliases.name: Tagger
aliases.comment:
Tagger holds the map function when Html.Map is used, the tagger
should then be applied to events comming from descendant nodes, it
is basically a javascript function.

values.name: decodeAttribute
values.comment:
Decode a JSON object into an Attribute. You have to pass a function that
decodes events from event attributes. If you don't want to decode event msgs,
you can ignore it:

    decodeAttribute (\_ -> ()) jsonHtml

If you do want to decode them, you will probably need to write some native code
like elm-html-test does to extract the function inside those.

values.name: decodeElmHtml
values.comment:
decode a json object into ElmHtml, you have to pass a function that decodes
events from Html Nodes. If you don't want to decode event msgs, you can ignore it:

decodeElmHtml (\_ _ -> ()) jsonHtml

if you do want to decode them, you will probably need to write some native code
like elm-html-test does to extract the function inside those.
values.type: (List Tagger -> EventHandler -> Decoder msg) -> Decoder (ElmHtml msg)

values.name: emptyFacts
values.comment: Just empty facts

values.name: toElementKind
values.comment:
Identify the kind of element. Helper to convert an tag name into a type for
pattern matching.

name: ElmHtml.Constants
comment:
Constants for representing internal keys for Elm's vdom implementation

@docs styleKey, eventKey, attributeKey, attributeNamespaceKey, knownKeys

values.name: attributeKey
values.comment: Internal key for style

values.name: attributeNamespaceKey
values.comment: Internal key for style

values.name: eventKey
values.comment: Internal key for style

values.name: knownKeys
values.comment: Keys that we are aware of and should pay attention to

values.name: styleKey
values.comment: Internal key for style

name: ElmHtml.Markdown

aliases.name: MarkdownOptions

values.name: baseMarkdownModel
values.comment: Just a default markdown model

values.name: decodeMarkdownModel
values.comment: decode a markdown model

values.name: encodeMarkdownModel
values.comment: encode markdown model

values.name: encodeOptions
values.comment:
We don't really care about encoding options right now
TODO: we will if we want to represent things as we do for elm-html
